visual_goal: Clean teaching transformation demo showing one parented three-segment robotic arm whose local joint rotations compose into the end-effector trajectory.
critic_priorities: preserve exactly one three-segment parented rig
make local coordinate frames readable at each joint
keep the end-effector trace thin, persistent, and visually central to the lesson
show formulas only as clean non-occluding teaching overlays
ensure motion comes from hierarchical rotations rather than free end-effector animation

must_have: one robot arm with exactly base, upper arm, forearm, and E marker
colored J1, J2, and E labels with thin RGB local axes attached
visible parent-child rotation behavior and growing E trajectory curves
theta_1 or theta_2 side panel only during the relevant joint rotation
required matrix formulas shown clearly when the storyboard calls for them
must_avoid: duplicated rigs or side-by-side comparison layouts
ghost before/after copies that imply separate arms
formula overlays covering the arm, joints, axes, or trace
paragraph text beyond J1, J2, E, theta_1, theta_2, and formulas
black void, factory setting, laser webs, or cluttered lighting props
aesthetic_goals: soft floor plane with neutral gradient background
readable colored joint spheres and axis arrows
smooth keyframed motion with stable three-quarter views
thin elegant trajectory curves that reveal transform composition
blocking_conditions: the rig is not a single parented hierarchy
segment count differs from exactly three rigid segments
local axes or required labels are missing or unreadable
end-effector trace is absent or appears hand-animated independently
required formula overlay is missing in formula shots

repair_policy: remove duplicate rigs, ghost arm copies, and before-after comparison props
restore Blender-style parent hierarchy: upper arm under base, forearm under upper arm, E under forearm
attach thin red X, green Y, and blue Z arrows to each joint frame and keep labels offset but readable
move formulas to upper-right or side-panel space if they overlap the arm or trace
replace thick trails with thin colored curve polylines that grow from the E marker
keep the scene on a soft floor with neutral background and simple soft key/rim lighting
spatial_relationships: base_segment is anchored at the floor origin
upper_arm_segment is parented to base_segment at joint_J1_sphere
forearm_segment is parented to upper_arm_segment at joint_J2_sphere
end_effector_E_marker is attached to the tip of forearm_segment
local_frame_J1 rotates with the base and upper arm subtree
local_frame_J2 and local_frame_E rotate with the forearm subtree
end_effector_trace grows from end_effector_E_marker and remains above soft_floor_plane
forbidden_abstractions: robot arm replaced by disconnected line segments
three-segment hierarchy replaced by a free-floating point trace
local coordinate frames drawn as global static axes only
J1 and J2 motion shown as independent unparented rotations
end-effector trajectory shown without visible joint-driven cause
formula lesson replaced by generic transformation arrows
single rig split into multiple comparison copies
